description: Carbon has four valence electrons, allowing it to bond with four other atoms.
The ability to form long carbon-carbon chains, branched chains, or rings.
Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements, e.g., structural isomers of butane.
Specific groups of atoms within molecules that are responsible for the characteristic chemical reactions of those molecules.
A family of organic compounds with the same functional group and similar chemical properties, differing by a CH2 unit.
